title: Compare Area and Perimeter
essential_question: How are area and perimeter different, and how do you choose which to use?

pretest.question: Which is the PERIMETER of a rectangle?
choices: C) Number of unit squares inside

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.11 Lesson 11.8 'Area and Perimeter Comparison'
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Topic D — Area vs Perimeter

pretest.question: You want to BUY FENCE to go around a yard. Area or perimeter?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.11 Lesson 11.8 'Area and Perimeter Comparison'
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Topic D — Area vs Perimeter
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Area vs Perimeter — The Big Difference
learn.content: AREA = the SPACE INSIDE a shape. Measured in SQUARE units. Use multiplication. PERIMETER = the DISTANCE AROUND the outside. Measured in regular (length) units. Use addition.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.11 Lesson 11.8 'Area and Perimeter Comparison'
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Topic D — Area vs Perimeter
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Different Units
learn.content: Area: SQUARE units (sq cm, sq m). Perimeter: regular length units (cm, m). The two measurements answer different questions, so they use different units.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.11 Lesson 11.8 'Area and Perimeter Comparison'
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Topic D — Area vs Perimeter
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: When to Use Area
learn.content: Use AREA when the question is about COVERING or FILLING. Examples: How much paint to cover a wall? How big is the floor for carpet? How much grass on a field?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.11 Lesson 11.8 'Area and Perimeter Comparison'
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Topic D — Area vs Perimeter
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: When to Use Perimeter
learn.content: Use PERIMETER when the question is about going AROUND or BORDERING. Examples: How much fence around a yard? How much string for a frame? How much trim around a window?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.11 Lesson 11.8 'Area and Perimeter Comparison'
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Topic D — Area vs Perimeter
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Same Shape, Different Measurements
learn.content: A 5 × 3 rectangle has: AREA = 5 × 3 = 15 sq cm. PERIMETER = 5 + 3 + 5 + 3 = 16 cm. Both numbers describe the same rectangle but measure different things.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.11 Lesson 11.8 'Area and Perimeter Comparison'
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Topic D — Area vs Perimeter
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Same Area, Different Perimeter
learn.content: Two rectangles can have the SAME AREA but DIFFERENT PERIMETERS. Example: a 2 × 6 has area 12 sq, perimeter 16. A 3 × 4 has area 12 sq, perimeter 14. Same area, different perimeter!

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.11 Lesson 11.8 'Area and Perimeter Comparison'
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Topic D — Area vs Perimeter
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Same Perimeter, Different Area
learn.content: Two rectangles can have the SAME PERIMETER but DIFFERENT AREAS. Example: a 1 × 5 has perimeter 12, area 5. A 3 × 3 has perimeter 12, area 9. Same perimeter, different area!

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.11 Lesson 11.8 'Area and Perimeter Comparison'
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Topic D — Area vs Perimeter
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Lesson Summary
learn.content: Today: (1) AREA = space inside (square units). (2) PERIMETER = distance around (regular units). (3) Use AREA for covering/filling, PERIMETER for fences/borders. (4) Same shape can have area and perimeter as DIFFERENT numbers. (5) Same area ≠ same perimeter; same perimeter ≠ same area.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.11 Lesson 11.8 'Area and Perimeter Comparison'
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Topic D — Area vs Perimeter
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
